notes: warnUnexplainedChanges writes only to os.Stderr, never touches stdout JSON, never changes the exit code, and the message is actionable (commit them or record with tp keep). Consistent with tp's warning convention (warnings not gated by --quiet).

Exit codes correct: hc-strategy reject ExitUsage(2) with actionable hcCommitHint (94-98); ExitFile(3)/ExitState(4)/ExitValidation(1) on the respective paths. output.Success (177) is stderr-only and suppressed in json mode, so output.JSON(task) (178) yields pure stdout JSON. Hints actionable.

Test correctly encodes the contract: hc rejects commit/auto-commit/bare-done with exit 2 and 'commit_strategy is hc' message (45-65), while a batch bad row is a failed row (exit 1, closed/failed/failures shape) never a commit-strategy exit 4 (77-92).
runTP harness always passes --json and asserts parseable JSON, correct status/counts, and exit codes across the lifecycle. Deterministic env (NO_COLOR=1, TP_HC=0). No contract issues.

hc-strategy reject uses ExitUsage(2) + hcCommitHint (44-47). Output {id,sha,message} matches documented shape (137-141); message is first line only (token-efficient). Fixed keys, deterministic. Error paths use correct ExitFile/ExitState codes.

Test encodes the commit_shas array + commit_sha mirror contract (27-28), duplicate sha exit 1 (40-45), covered-by records neither (nil, consistent with omitempty commit_shas / null commit_sha, 48-59), reopen clears both, set rejects managed field exit 2. Contract-correct.

hcCommitHint const is actionable (13). warnCommitStrategy emits unrecognized-value and hc-absent warnings to os.Stderr only, never changing the exit code (32-41). No JSON stdout surface; supports the contract cleanly.

Both output shapes deterministic (map keys sorted by encoding/json). New keys commit_strategy/commit_strategy_effective are additive, normalized (never null). Verified wf.Checks defaults to []model.Check{} (engine.DefaultWorkflow), so 'checks' serializes as [] not null. --compact documented no-op. Warnings to stderr only.

Test verifies the {value,source} resolved-config contract: a clamped out-of-range override attributes to 'project' not 'override' (16-32), and an unrecognized commit_strategy reports source 'override' with resolved value 'builtin' (37-49). Contract-correct.

Empty collections handled: closedIDs/failed are make([]..,0) => [] not null (382-383); batch 'failures' conditionally included so absent-not-null. Documented shapes match (single 319-336, multi 526-542, batch 803-815). Error JSON {error,code,acceptance,hint} actionable (233-243). Exit codes 1/2/3/4 correct incl hc reject ExitUsage(2) 127-138. Ordering deterministic (arg/toposort order).

R1 fixes verified. NormalizeKeepPath error now handled: on err, skip falls back to the given taskFilePath (L30-33). ClassifyPaths malformed-keep fail-safe documented: on err, changes stays = raw so every change is over-reported, never swallowed (L20-25). No task-file write here; no lock needed.

WithFileLock (L128) wraps the sole WriteTaskFile (L169). Pre-flock read/find/verify errors all surface via output.Error+os.Exit; state is re-read and re-validated inside the lock (L129-154, §6.2). No swallowed errors, no lock-free write path.
Test file. os.WriteFile uses 0o600 + filepath.Join, wrapped in require.NoError; temp dir via t.TempDir (auto-clean). No unpaired lock, no swallowed error.
Test file. os.WriteFile with 0o600 + filepath.Join + error assertion; t.TempDir for isolation. Clean.

R1 fix verified. Lock-file unstage now uses `git rm --cached --ignore-unmatch -q -- *.lock ...` (HEAD-independent, no-match safe), documented best-effort so ignoring its result cannot leave a lock staged (L104-108). WithFileLock (L50) wraps WriteTaskFile (L131). git args passed as exec argv (no shell injection).
Test file. os.WriteFile 0o600 + filepath.Join(dir,...) + require.NoError; dir from setupCommitProject helper (temp). Clean.

R1 fix verified. Dropped LoadTaskWorkflowOverride error documented as intentional (L19-22): a parse error yields the zero WorkflowOverride and the command's own model.ReadTaskFile aborts exit 3 on a truly malformed file, losing no safety. Returns value structs, so taskOverride.CommitStrategy (*string field) is nil-safe to read/pass.

Safe. L107 drops LoadTaskWorkflowOverride's error like commitstrategy.go but lacks the parity inline comment; not a safety gap because ResolveEffectiveWorkflow (L109) re-reads/validates the same file and aborts exit 3 on malformed config (L110-118), and DiscoverTaskFile's error IS checked (L106). Nit only: add the one-line rationale for consistency. Read-only resolution; no lock needed.
Test file. os.WriteFile 0o600 + filepath.Join + require.NoError; .tp/config.json and task file written under t.TempDir. Clean.

readBatchEntries (L895-916) never checks scanner.Err() after the scan loop (L904-914): a read I/O error or bufio.ErrTooLong (an NDJSON line exceeding the 64KB default token, plausible with commit_shas arrays) silently ends the loop and returns the partial entries with nil error, so `tp done --batch` could close only a prefix of tasks yet report success. Add `if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil { return nil, err }`. Everything else is solid: json.Unmarshal errors ARE caught (L910-912); all three write paths (L279/495/769) are inside WithFileLock; json.Marshal of a controlled map at L239 is the accepted never-fails idiom.
Both helpers have clear doc comments referencing §8.3; dropped errors (ClassifyPaths, NormalizeKeepPath) are fail-safe with explanatory comments; no nil-slice-to-JSON, no magic strings, no dead code.

New hc-strategy guard (94-98) reuses hcCommitHint const and engine.CommitStrategyHC; follows the established pre-flock/in-flock double-check pattern. runClose is long (~144 lines) but consistent with tp's other cobra handlers and pre-existing; not a categorizable defect.

setupCloseStrategyProject helper documented; test names descriptive and follow TestX_Scenario pattern; assertions check exit codes and the 'commit_strategy is hc' message consistently. No duplication issues.
Pre-existing test scaffolding (TestMain, runTP helper, lifecycle tests); no v0.28.0-specific additions. Naming and structure consistent with codebase test conventions.

New hc guard (44-48) reuses hcCommitHint/CommitStrategyHC; SetCommitSHAs used (129). Helpers extracted (buildCommitMessage, gitStage, gitCommit); gitStage/gitCommit wrap with %w. runCommit ~107 lines, within cobra tolerance. Clean.

Covers commit_shas/commit_sha mirror, dedup exit-1, covered-by records none, reopen clears, managed-field rejection. taskState helper trivial (no doc comment, consistent with test-helper norms). Well-named, no dead code.

Confirmed warnCommitStrategy now returns bare 'string' (no unused named return); resolveEffectiveStrategy drops LoadTaskWorkflowOverride error with a documented fail-safe rationale. hcCommitHint is a named const; §5.2/5.3/5.4 doc comments present. Clean.

Every function has a §-referenced doc comment; malformed config handled via errors.As + MalformedConfigError.Hint(); maps sized with make(). Minor: engine.ProjectWorkflowOverride('.') read again at L136 after resolveConfigWorkflow, a cheap redundant read, not a defect.
Two focused tests (clamped-source attribution §3.4/§10.8, commit_strategy source §10.7) with doc comments and clear assertion messages. Consistent naming, no duplication.

New resolveCommitSHAs is documented, uses make([]string,0,...) and returns a clear dedup error; hc guards (127-138,745-748) reuse hcCommitHint/CommitStrategyHC; SetCommitSHAs used. closedIDs/failed use make() (no null-vs-[] bug). runDoneSingle/Multi/Batch exceed the ~110 cobra guideline but this is pre-existing and consistent across tp; not a categorizable defect.
tp resume oracle: AssembleResume reads durable state (task file, .tp-review/, .tp/local.json, git) and reports phase + next action; command in cli/resume.go.
commit_strategy gains real behavior: Resolve/Effective strategy; delegation to hc while tp keeps the task-commit record (commit_shas).
keep_uncommitted []KeepEntry {path,reason} durable in .tp/local.json; the cross-reset memory of intentional leftovers.
AssembleResume performs no write (read-only); tp ships no driver — orchestrator resets, tp only reconstructs state from disk.
Forgotten work surfaces as unexplained-changes blocker; keep-list (keeplistmatch.go) is clarity not safety.
KeepEntry.reason + commit_shas (task.go:34) + closed_reason are the structured note the finishing agent leaves; resume reads them.
warnUnexplainedChanges reports and never commits/discards; resume is read-only; tp never runs hc — report, do not force.
HasHC only probes PATH (TP_HC override for tests); tp never invokes hc — hc is an optional auto-detected peer, not a hard requirement.
phase field (one of review/decompose/implement/audit/release, phase.go 4-9) in ResumeResult JSON.
spec field: the resolved spec path string in ResumeResult.
changes: unexplained paths not on keep-list, byte-sorted (sort.Strings); worktree.go 24-26 returns empty for non-git dir.
kept: []KeepEntry {path,reason} (matched file not glob, first-match reason via keeplistmatch.go 48-49), byte-sorted on path.
next_action {command,summary,payload}; command *string is nil for decompose/release (BuildNextAction 170-176).
blockers array of {code,class,message,data}; empty (not nil) when clear (BuildBlockers 49); --compact strips message in cli/resume.go 104-111.
builtin: tp performs the commit itself (commit.go/done --auto-commit/--commit all work).
hc: tp performs no commit; tp commit and --auto-commit rejected; close records SHAs via tp done --commit.
auto: resolves to hc when HasHC() else builtin (EffectiveCommitStrategy).
No command clears/compacts/spawns context; resume is read-only. No reset command exists in the codebase.
No driver binary and no runtime-specific integration ship in tp; the reference driver is documentation only.
resume reads existing Converged/StateStale from .tp-review state; convergence computation is unchanged by this release.
commit_strategy is in readOnlyWorkflowFields — tp set --workflow rejects it; authored only by tp init/.tp/config.json.
tp neither invokes hc nor computes hunk assignments; HasHC only probes PATH.
resolveCommitSHAs accepts a supplied list (dedups, orders); it never generates/splits a diff.
Under hc tp authors no commit message (no gitCommit call on the hc path); the task-commit link is commit_shas, no message-scaffold command.
No hard clean-tree gate; tp warns and never commits or discards; keeping a file is recorded in the keep-list, not enforced.
Structured KeepEntry.reason only; no free-text hand-off file exists in the state model.
keep-list lives in git-ignored .tp/local.json (EnsureTPGitignore keeps local.json ignored); repo-scoped, not spec-scoped.
Matching is filepath.Match (no ** recursive glob); documented and enforced by the stdlib matcher.
Exactly 5 phases; release is terminal — no persisted 'done' lifecycle marker field exists.
Review-converged, zero tasks -> decompose (review loop finished); reset boundary realized as a phase transition.
Between review rounds: review phase; next_action re-runs tp review (resumepayload.go 145-151).
decompose phase: null command, {} payload — tp import closes the phase; boundary reset is orchestrator-driven.
Open task -> implement; next_action is tp next (resumepayload.go 159-169). Between-task reset boundary.
All tasks done, audit unconverged -> audit; the implementation-loop boundary.
Between audit rounds: audit phase; next_action re-runs tp audit (resumepayload.go 152-158).
Audit converged -> release, null command (resumepayload.go 172-173); no reset — human-approved release is terminal.
unexplained-changes: agent-clearable, fires when changes non-empty, data {count}.
no-ready-task: escalate, implement with open-but-none-ready, data {blocked_by} from unmetDeps.
review-budget-exhausted: escalate, ReviewMaxRounds!=0 && rounds>=cap && !converged; cap 0 never fires. data {cap}.
audit-budget-exhausted: escalate, phase-gated, AuditMaxRounds!=0 guard; data {cap}.
spec-stale: escalate, ReviewStale && phase in {implement,audit}; data {spec}.

CommitSHAs []string omitempty (34); UnmarshalJSON mirrors commit_sha=shas[0] when non-empty (88-92); SetCommitSHAs (100-109); a pre-0.28.0 task with only commit_sha leaves CommitSHAs nil (omitted), reads back unchanged.

Plain tp config adds top-level commit_strategy_effective (builtin/hc); --resolved keeps commit_strategy {value,source} (57-60); warnCommitStrategy (commitstrategy.go 32-41) prints stderr warning for unrecognized value and hc-absent.

CLAUDE.md 213 updates the self-development rule for the hc close flow; SKILL.md (tp resume x4, tp keep x5), README.md (tp resume x11, tp keep x7, reference driver), skills/tp/REFERENCE.md (tp resume x4, tp keep x5, commit_strategy x4) document the new commands/strategy/driver.
--commit repeatable via resolveCommitSHAs (dup -> exit 1); SetCommitSHAs records ordered list & mirrors commit_sha[0] (274-276); --covered-by records neither (guarded by !isCoveredBy).

unexplainedChangeCount classifies against keep-list, excludes tp's own task-file/lock write; warnUnexplainedChanges prints one-line stderr warning, never changes exit code, never commits/discards. Called in done.go 285 and close.go 175.

tp keep add/update, --remove (absent path no-op exit 0), --list ([] when empty); missing reason exit 2 (57-60); malformed glob exit 2 (63-66); path NormalizeKeepPath repo-root-relative from any subdir.
MatchKeepList uses filepath.Match, first matching entry wins and supplies reason, malformed pattern returns ErrBadPattern; ClassifyPaths splits into Kept {path,reason} and Changes.
LocalConfig.KeepUncommitted *[]KeepEntry omitempty: nil marshals omitted, non-nil empty marshals []; KeepEntry {path,reason}.

UpdateKeepList reads/writes .tp/local.json under WithFileLock; LoadLocalConfig (projectconfig.go 214-224) round-trips presence (absent stays nil, explicit empty stays []); paths normalized repo-root-relative by NormalizeKeepPath 19-33.
commit_shas in managedFields map -> tp set rejects exit 2; reopen.go 58-59 clears CommitSHA and CommitSHAs alongside gate_passed_at/gate_skipped_reason (57,60).
DetectPhase task-first ordering: open task -> implement even when stale (staleness only adds blocker); zero tasks review-converged -> decompose else review.
AssembleResume returns {phase,spec,changes,kept,next_action,blockers}; changes/kept byte-sorted (38-39); no write performed.
BuildBlockers emits {code,class,message,data} in fixed order; unexplained-changes agent-clearable, others escalate; budget blockers phase-gated and cap!=0-gated.

tp resume: spec arg wins (34-43), exit 3 when no task file (46-51), JSON when piped (75-77), --compact strips summary/reason/message keeping data (86-111), read-only. Spec resolution via engine.ResolveSpecPath (discover.go) and SpecAdjacentTaskFile (workflow_resolve.go 72).
BuildNextAction: review/audit round=len(rounds)+1 with 0 unresolved on first round; implement previews {task,wip}; decompose/release nil command, {} payload.

Effective hc gating: tp commit (commit.go 44-48), --auto-commit and bare done (done.go 128-137), tp close (close.go 94-98) rejected exit 2 with hcCommitHint; batch bad row is a failed row (745-746); no exit-4 path.
ResolveCommitStrategy: task>project>auto default; present unrecognized -> builtin+warning, absent -> auto; EffectiveCommitStrategy auto->hc when HasHC else builtin.
Asserts code/class/data shapes, agent-clearable vs escalate, fixed emission order, budget-exhausted at cap, cap-0 never fires, clean is empty-not-nil.
Asserts builtin commit/auto-commit close; hc rejects commit/auto-commit/bare-done exit 2 (not 4); hc closes with --commit/--covered-by; batch bad row is a failed row (exit 1).
Asserts two-commit record+mirror, single, duplicate exit 1 (task stays open), covered-by records none, set rejects managed, reopen clears, builtin commit records single-element list.
Asserts --compact strips summary/kept.reason/blocker.message while keeping payload/path/data; resume writes no file (task file byte-identical, no .tp/.tp-review created) and emits JSON.
Asserts no-task-file+no-arg exit 3; spec arg wins over discovered spec; absent adjacent task file yields review.
Asserts unexplained change -> exit 0 + stderr count, change stays unmodified, keep-listed change produces no warning.
Asserts add/overwrite/remove/list-[], missing reason exit 2, malformed glob exit 2, subdir path stored repo-root-relative, writes only .tp/local.json (no task/.tp-review file).
Asserts implement prefers-wip/first-ready/null+no-ready-task blocker, review/audit round=1 unresolved=0 with command literals, decompose/release nil command & empty payload.
TestDetectPhase table + AssembleResume tests assert implement-when-stale (with spec-stale blocker), audit, release, decompose, review transitions from durable state.
TestResolveCommitStrategy asserts precedence, auto default, unrecognized->builtin+warning; TestEffectiveCommitStrategy asserts auto/hc/builtin effective + hcMissing flag.
Asserts modified/staged/untracked in changes with unexplained-changes count, staged rename dest, tp keep moves path to kept, glob matches two files (two kept entries), non-git dir empty arrays.
git status --porcelain=v1 -z -uall run at repo root; parsePorcelainZ yields dest of staged rename (skips origin on R/C), untracked enumerated individually; non-git dir -> empty slice.
